title: Trichophyllum torreyanum
taxonomy: Trichophyllum torreyanum is a species of moss in the family Bryaceae. It is a type of pleurocarpous moss, characterized by its branching stems and leaves that grow in a two-ranked arrangement. The classification of Trichophyllum torreyanum includes the kingdom Plantae, phylum Bryophyta, class Bryopsida, order Bryales, and family Bryaceae. This species is native to North America, specifically the western regions of the continent. Further classification may include the genus Trichophyllum and the species T. torreyanum.
uses: Trichophyllum torreyanum is a species of moss, and there isn't much information available about its medicinal uses. However, mosses in general have been used in traditional medicine for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Some species of moss have been studied for their antioxidant and antifungal properties, which could have applications in treating various health conditions. Further research is needed to determine the specific medicinal uses of Trichophyllum torreyanum. Its potential uses may include treating skin conditions, wound healing, or other applications where antimicrobial or anti-inflammatory properties are beneficial.

preparations: Trichophyllum torreyanum, also known as the mountain panise, has been used in traditional herbal medicine.
